title: WGA Aims to Turn Focus to Studios, While Staff Remains on Strike
content:
ByGene Maddaus

Senior Media Writer

The Writers Guild of America issued its opening salvo the 2026 studio negotiations on Thursday, even as its own staff continues to walk a picket line outside union headquarters.

The union is preparing to sit down with the Alliance of Motion Picture and Television Producers on March 16. In a bulletin to members on Thursday,WGAleadership offered an update on the “state of the industry,” arguing that profits are rebounding and studios can afford to make a “fair deal.”

“Every three years during MBA negotiations, the companies will point to some part of their business that is not performing well to claim they couldn’t possibly meet writers’ needs,” the uniontold its members. “This cycle is no different.”

The WGA has said that negotiations will go on as usual, even if the staff remains on strike. The Writers Guild Staff Union, which represents about two-thirds of all WGA West employees, walked out on Feb. 17, alleging that WGA West leadership had failed to bargain in good faith on issues like wage scales and just cause for discipline. The union has warned that the Writers Guild Awards, scheduled for March 8, could be canceled if a deal is not reached soon.

The WGA has long issued state-of-the-industry reports at the beginning of anAMPTPnegotiating cycle. The 2017 reporttouted“unprecedented prosperity”; the 2023 reportarguedthat the fundamentals of the business remained strong despite a decline in profits.

The primary concern in this round of negotiations is the WGA health plan, whichlost$122 million in the just two years according to its tax returns. The plan is suffering both due to a decline in contributions — owing to a downturn in employment — and an increase in health care costs.

The AMPTPissued a reportin December highlighting the high costs of the health plans of the WGA, SAG-AFTRA and the Directors Guild of America, compared to other multi-employer plans.

“These health plans offer top-tier benefits, including zero or low participant premiums, minimal cost-sharing, low dependent costs, comprehensive prescription coverage, and early retiree eligibility,” the studio group stated. “AMPTP Member Companies remain committed to partnering with the Guilds to ensure their health plans are properly structured and funded.”

The WGA contract is set to expire on May 1. The studios are expected to make a significant contribution toward the health fund as part of any deal. A key question is whether the WGA would be open to any curtailment of benefits, potentially in the form of higher premiums and deductibles.

The WGA report did not touch on health care.

“As streaming continues its upward ascent, the media companies can afford to negotiate a fair deal with writers,” the union stated.
title_zh: 編劇工會(WGA)旨在將焦點轉向製片廠，而員工仍然在罷工中。
content_zh:
由基恩·馬達斯(Gene Maddaus)報導

高級媒體撰稿人

美國編劇工會(Writers Guild of America, WGA)在週四發出了2026年影視製作公司協商的開場信號，儘管其員工仍在工會總部外進行罷工。

該工會準備於3月16日與影視製作公司聯盟(Alliance of Motion Picture and Television Producers)進行會談。在週四發給會員的公告中，WGA領導層提供了“行業狀況”的最新消息，並主張利潤正在回升，製作公司可以負擔得起“公平的交易”。

“每三年在MBA協商期間，這些公司總會指向其業務中某些表現不佳的部分，聲稱他們無法滿足編劇的需求，”工會告訴其會員。“這次的情況也不例外。”

WGA表示，即使員工仍在罷工，協商將照常進行。代表約三分之二WGA西部(WGA West)員工的編劇工會員工工會(Writers Guild Staff Union)於2月17日罷工，指控WGA西部領導層在工資標準和紀律正當理由等問題上未能進行誠信談判。工會警告，如果未能迅速達成協議，定於3月8日舉行的編劇工會獎(Writers Guild Awards)可能會被取消。

WGA長期以來在AMPTP協商周期開始時發佈行業狀況報告。2017年的報告宣稱“前所未有的繁榮”；2023年的報告則主張儘管利潤下降，業務基本面仍然強勁。

此次協商的主要關注點是WGA健康計劃，根據其稅務申報，該計劃在短短兩年內虧損了1.22億美元。該計劃受到貢獻減少（由於就業下降）和醫療成本上升的影響。

AMPTP在12月發佈了一份報告，強調WGA、SAG-AFTRA和美國導演工會(Directors Guild of America)的健康計劃與其他多雇主計劃相比的高成本。

“這些健康計劃提供頂級福利，包括零或低參與者保費、最低的成本分擔、低依賴者成本、全面的處方覆蓋以及提前退休資格，”該製作公司集團表示。“AMPTP成員公司仍然致力於與工會合作，以確保其健康計劃得到適當的結構和資金支持。”

WGA合約將於5月1日到期。製作公司預計將在任何交易中對健康基金做出重大貢獻。一個關鍵問題是WGA是否會對任何福利的削減持開放態度，這可能以提高保費和免賠額的形式出現。

WGA報告並未觸及醫療保健問題。

“隨著串流媒體不斷上升，媒體公司可以負擔得起與編劇進行公平交易的談判，”工會表示。
summary_zh:
由基恩·馬達斯(Gene Maddaus)報導

高級媒體撰稿人

美國編劇工會(Writers Guild of America, WGA)在週四發出了2026年影視製作公司協商的開場信號，儘管其員工仍在工會總部外進行罷工。

該工會準備於3月16日與影視製作公司聯盟(Alliance of Motion Picture and Television Producers)進行會談。
